articles.title: RBI allows banks to extend relief measures to borrowers without their requests in disaster-hit areas
articles.summary: New Reserve Bank of India guidelines empower banks to offer relief to all borrowers in calamity-hit regions. These measures, effective July 1, 2026, can be extended without borrower requests. Banks can also operate from temporary premises and set up satellite offices. ATM services will be restored promptly. Relief can include fee waivers for up to a year.

articles.title: The rise of infrastructure for digital censorshipA series of IT Rules amendments has shifted online speech control from courts to the executive, enabling fast, opaque takedowns with limited accountability.12 April, 20266 min readApar GuptaNikhil Pahwa
articles.summary: A series of amendments to India's IT Rules has transferred control over online speech from the judiciary to the executive branch. This change allows for rapid, opaque content takedowns with minimal oversight. The Internet Freedom Foundation highlights concerns over accountability and free speech implications. The amendments mark a shift in how digital censorship is enforced in India.

articles.title: SC Upholds Odomos Classification as Mosquito Repellent based on Common Parlance Test
articles.summary: The Supreme Court (SC) upheld the classification of Odomos, a mosquito repellent product, under the Harmonized System of Nomenclature (HSN) code 38089191. The decision was based on the 'common parlance test,' which considers market identity and consumer understanding. The SC's ruling supports the product's treatment as a mosquito repellent for customs and regulatory purposes.
articles.raw_summary: SC allowed classification of Odomos under HSN 38089191 to stand, holding that market identity and consumer understanding supported its treatment as a mosquito repellent. articles.summary: The Indian government has waived penalties for contractors and suppliers engaged in public procurement (goods, services, and works) with government agencies. The move aims to mitigate disruptions caused by the crisis for public sector projects.
articles.raw_summary: The relief, however, applies to contracts with government agencies across goods, services and works, covering suppliers, consultants and contractors engaged in public procurement.

articles.title: Supreme Court To Hear Kannur Corporation's Challenge To Kerala High Court Order Silencing Its Siren

articles.title: How a law to empower Adivasis is being used to target Christians in Chhattisgarh
articles.summary: The Panchayats (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act (PESA) in Chhattisgarh, designed to empower Adivasis, is being misused to target Christians. While land rights provisions of PESA are rarely enforced, its cultural protection clauses are invoked to ostracize Christian communities. This misuse raises concerns about the law's implementation and its impact on minority rights in tribal areas.
articles.raw_summary: PESA provisions about land rights are hardly used, many say. But its clauses about cultural protections are invoked to ostracise those who follow Christianity.

articles.title: Preventive Detention Without Trial: Law, Practice and Abuse
articles.summary: The article examines India's laws on preventive detention without trial, balancing constitutional protections with broad government powers. It argues that existing procedural safeguards are insufficient to protect individual rights and calls for stronger legal protections. The discussion focuses on the balance between national security needs and fundamental freedoms, particularly in cases where detention occurs without judicial review.
articles.raw_summary: The issue highlights constitutional protections alongside broad detention powers. The takeaway is that procedural safeguards must be strengthened to ensure real protection of rights. The post Preventive Detention Without Trial: Law, Practice and Abuse appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Employees Not Liable for GST Penalty in Absence of Taxable Person Status: Bombay HC
articles.summary: The Bombay High Court ruled that employees cannot be penalized under Section 122(1A) of the GST Act for company-level violations unless they are registered as 'taxable persons.' The court held that only those with independent GST registration can be held liable, excluding regular employees. This decision clarifies the scope of liability for GST violations and impacts how companies and employees may be treated in such cases.
articles.raw_summary: The Court examined whether employees could be penalised under Section 122(1A) for company-level GST violations. articles.title: Loose Sheets and Digital Data Not Enough: ITAT Quashes Additions for Lack of Evidence
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that tax authorities cannot make additions to a taxpayer's income based solely on loose sheets or digital data seized from third parties unless there is a clear link to the assessee. The tribunal emphasized that third-party documents without corroboration lack evidentiary value. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle evidence in assessments and may influence future tax policy and enforcement practices.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that additions cannot stand without a clear link between seized material and the assessee. It ruled that third-party documents without corroboration lack evidentiary value. The post Loose Sheets and Digital Data Not Enough: ITAT Quashes Additions for Lack of Evidence appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Gold Monetisation Scheme 2026: Earn Interest on Idle Gold at Home
articles.summary: The Gold Monetisation Scheme 2026 allows households to deposit idle gold with banks or institutions to earn interest without selling it. Both the interest earned and capital gains from the scheme are fully tax-exempt. The scheme is aimed at monetizing unused gold holdings in Indian households while providing financial benefits to depositors.

articles.title: What Noida's factory protests mean for India's wage debate
articles.summary: Factory workers' protests in Noida have reignited India's minimum wage debate. Rising inflation erodes real wages, impacting workers' purchasing power. States show varied wage growth, with some narrowing gaps but disparities persist. Households increasingly rely on borrowing. A granular approach to minimum wages and support for migrant workers are crucial. Targeted support can help MSMEs manage wage hikes.

articles.title: DRP 270426: Probe into violations by Pune River Front Development Project
articles.summary: The Pune River Front Development Project is under investigation for allegedly violating environmental clearances by significantly narrowing the river's cross-section along its 44-km length, which could compromise its flood-handling capacity. The state has ordered that the complainant be included in site visits and consultative meetings. The complaint highlights that the project's design defies environmental norms despite higher potential flood risks. The state's directive indicates an official p
articles.raw_summary: The complaint argues that the project's design significantly reduces the river's cross-section across its entire 44-km length, drastically compromising its ability to handle floodwaters. "While actual potential flood levels are significantly higher, the project is narrowing the river in defiance of environmental clearances," he added. The state's order specifies that the complainant must be included in the upcoming site visits and all relevant consultative meetings.

articles.title: Human Rights Commissions Ignore Lynching of Muslims: Hon’ble Mr Justice Atul Sreedharan
articles.summary: The article reports on a court examination of whether the National Human Rights Commission (NHRC) can direct investigations into cases unrelated to human rights violations. The court ruled that such actions may exceed the NHRC's statutory limits under existing law. The ruling highlights concerns about the NHRC's role in addressing lynching incidents targeting Muslims, raising questions about its effectiveness and authority in protecting civil liberties.

articles.title: The Plant Fibre Sector Keeps Chasing Visibility When It Needs Markets
articles.summary: The plant fibre sector in India is gaining attention as industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact. However, output remains low due to fragmented supply chains, weak processing capacity, and uncertain demand. A FIBRAL report suggests that diversification will require standards, investment, and market architecture, not just increased fibre production. The sector's struggles highlight potential areas for policy intervention to boost sustainable alternatives.
articles.raw_summary: As industries seek bio-based materials with lower environmental impact, alternative plant fibres are drawing new policy and commercial attention. Yet output remains marginal and forecast to contract, constrained by fragmented chains, weak processing capacity and uncertain offtake. A FIBRAL report argues diversification will depend on standards, investment and market architecture, not fibre discovery alone at scale.

As part of the order, the director was directed to deposit Rs 20,000 in PGIMER Chandigarh's Poor Patient Welfare Fund within a month.

articles.title: Oil falls as Iran proposes talks but prices still set for weekly gains

Persistent geopolitical tensions around the Strait of Hormuz and export restrictions kept markets volatile. Traders reacted sharply to headlines despite a fragile ceasefire. The situation highlights India's vulnerability to global oil market fluctuations and potential policy responses.

Ongoing tensions around the Strait of Hormuz and geopolitical uncertainty kept markets volatile, with traders reacting sharply to headlines despite a fragile ceasefire and continued export restrictions.

articles.title: FinMin’s Expenditure Secy Vualnam flags 'very difficult' days ahead
articles.summary: Expenditure Secretary V. Vualnam of India's Finance Ministry warned of 'very difficult' days ahead due to multiple economic stress points and limited visibility following the West Asia crisis. He stressed that fiscal discipline has strengthened the government's position and reiterated that capital expenditure will remain a priority despite rising pressures.

Vualnam warned that the outlook is “very difficult” with multiple stress points and limited visibility, even as he said fiscal discipline has put the government on a strong footing and reiterated that capital expenditure will remain a priority despite rising pressures.

articles.title: Australian banks warned frontier AI could create larger, faster cyber attacks

Current cybersecurity measures in banks are struggling to keep up with AI's rapid evolution, increasing risks to the financial system. The regulator urges board members to better understand AI-related risks to mitigate these threats.

Current cybersecurity measures within banks are trailing behind the swift evolution of AI, heightening the vulnerability of the country’s financial systems. It is crucial for board members to enhance their grasp of AI-related risks.

articles.title: Teen rape survivor’s choice vs unborn baby’s right to life—how SC’s new order deviates from precedent
articles.summary: The Supreme Court, led by Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, rejected AIIMS' plea to deny a 15-year-old rape survivor the right to terminate her 27-week pregnancy. Earlier court rulings often blocked terminations if the fetus was healthy, but this order deviates from precedent. The decision highlights the conflict between a survivor's choice and the unborn baby's right to life. The case underscores the need for clearer legal frameworks on abortion rights in India.
articles.raw_summary: SC bench led by CJI Surya Kant rejected AIIMS plea against allowing a 15-year-old girl to terminate her 27-week pregnancy. Earlier rulings often denied termination if foetus was in good health.

articles.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs (CBIC) revised customs tariff values for gold, silver, and edible oils, effective from May 1, 2026. The update aims to align customs valuation with current market prices for accurate duty assessment. The decision impacts import costs and trade flows for these key commodities. The CBIC is the regulatory body responsible for customs and indirect tax administration in India.
articles.raw_summary: CBIC updated tariff values for key commodities including oils, gold, and silver. The revision ensures accurate customs valuation aligned with current market prices. articles.summary: The Centre and state governments collected a record ₹2.43 trillion in gross GST revenue in April, up from ₹2.37 trillion last year. articles.raw_summary: Last April, the Centre and states had collected ₹2.37 trillion in GST revenue before adjusting for refunds, but the figures are not strictly comparable because of the GST cuts in September 2025.

articles.title: Wife Can't Seek Husband's Income Tax Details Under RTI Act To Support Maintenance Claim: Delhi High Court
articles.summary: The Delhi High Court has held that income tax details of a husband cannot be disclosed under the Right to Information Act, 2005 in relation to the matrimonial disputes with the wife. Justice Purushaindra Kumar Kaurav observed that such information does not fall under the exception of “larger public interest” under the enactment. The Court made the observations while setting aside an order of the Central Information Commission (CIC) which had directed disclosure of a husband's net taxable income

articles.title: EV founders say PLI Auto needs an overhaul, leaves startup at a disadvantage
articles.summary: Electric vehicle startups are pushing back against the current production-linked incentive (PLI) scheme for automobiles, arguing its stringent eligibility criteria favor established players. EV-first companies, despite leading in technology and localization, are excluded, facing a significant cost disadvantage against traditional OEMs. Founders call for reforms to align the scheme with India's evolving EV landscape.

articles.title: Downside growth risks outweigh inflation concerns, RBI likely to hold rates this fiscal: Crisil
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India's Monetary Policy Committee is expected to hold interest rates steady this fiscal year. Economic growth concerns are seen as more pressing than inflation risks. Producers face higher costs, but consumer prices are contained. The government aims to reduce its fiscal deficit. Market borrowing will increase. The current account deficit is projected to widen.

articles.summary: Kerala rubber planters are opposing a proposed duty cut on natural rubber, warning it could harm farmers' livelihoods. Despite rubber prices being above ₹250 per kg—highest in 14 years—rising production costs (₹210-220 per kg) are squeezing farmers' profits. The planters argue the duty cut would further reduce their earnings, urging the government to reconsider the policy.

articles.title: Insurance: Increasing coverage, growing distress
articles.summary: Rising insurance coverage in India has not led to reduced hospitalisation or out-of-pocket healthcare expenses. Instead, more people are shifting to costlier private sector care. Government-funded health insurance schemes are failing to protect households from financial distress and are disproportionately benefiting wealthier sections of society.

This shift aligns with India's push for seamless, high-frequency digital transactions, including the surge in UPI usage.

The case raises questions about the impartiality of poll observers and the integrity of the electoral process.

articles.title: India invokes WTO peace clause for FY25 rice subsidies for farmers
articles.summary: India has once again utilized the World Trade Organisation peace clause. This is the seventh time the country has invoked this provision. It allows India to exceed the 10% subsidy limit for rice farmers. The government informed the WTO about providing subsidies worth $7.6 billion. This represents about 11.85% of the total value of rice production.

articles.title: UP introduces 10-year ‘warranty’ model for rural water schemes under Jal Jeevan Mission
articles.summary: The Uttar Pradesh government has launched a 10-year 'warranty' model for rural water schemes under the central Jal Jeevan Mission. Under this framework, construction agencies will be responsible for operating and maintaining water supply infrastructure for a decade. This aims to ensure long-term sustainability of rural water supply systems. The initiative is part of broader efforts to improve water access in rural areas.
articles.raw_summary: Under the new framework, construction agencies will remain responsible for operating and maintaining water supply infrastructure for a decade.

articles.title: Supreme Court Grants Anticipatory Bail To Pawan Khera In Assam Police FIR
articles.summary: The Supreme Court granted anticipatory bail to Pawan Khera in an Assam Police FIR, stating the allegations appeared to be politically motivated and did not require custodial interrogation. The ruling highlights concerns over potential misuse of legal processes for political purposes. The decision underscores the judiciary's role in protecting individuals from politically driven legal actions.
articles.raw_summary: The Court said that the allegations appear to be "politically motivated" which does not require a custodial interrogation.

articles.title: Why rejuvenating traditional water systems matters for Rajasthan
articles.summary: The article by Moumita Mukherjee of A.T.E. Chandra Foundation emphasizes the importance of reviving traditional water systems in Rajasthan. It argues that these systems are crucial for climate resilience and sustainable water management in the state. The piece suggests that neglecting these systems could worsen water scarcity, impacting agriculture and livelihoods. It calls for policy and community action to restore and maintain these heritage water structures.
articles.raw_summary: This article is authored by Moumita Mukherjee, deputy manager, A.T.E. Chandra Foundation.

This trend reflects increasing interest in streamlining treasury operations within India's financial ecosystem.

articles.summary: The European Union has charged Meta Platforms (owner of Facebook and Instagram) under the Digital Services Act for failing to adequately block under-13 users from accessing its platforms. Regulators cited weak safeguards as a concern for child safety online. This action follows growing scrutiny of social media platforms' role in protecting minors. The case reflects broader global efforts to regulate digital platforms and ensure child safety in the online space.

articles.title: FSSAI plastic-free push for pan masala may disrupt sachet economics, shift to paper formats
articles.summary: The Food Safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has proposed banning plastic and aluminium sachets for pan masala products, pushing manufacturers to use paper or other natural materials. This move aims to reduce plastic waste and align with environmental goals but may increase production costs and disrupt existing supply chains. The proposal reflects FSSAI's broader push for plastic-free packaging in the food industry.

articles.summary: Bengaluru exporters are poised to thrive as the India-Middle East-Europe Economic Corridor gains traction. articles.title: In 4 months, FPI selling in stocks surpasses $20 billion
articles.summary: Foreign investors have offloaded a record $20.6 billion in Indian stocks this year, exceeding 2025's highest-ever annual outflow. The recent West Asian conflict triggered a significant sell-off, with nearly $18.2 billion exiting since the war began. Factors like rising crude oil prices, a weakening rupee, and concerns over company growth and valuations are driving this trend.

articles.title: Proxy advisory firm asks RBI to reject Tata Sons’ application

Malhotra also highlighted the need for improved liquidity in government securities and expanded access to the FX retail platform for fair customer deals.

articles.title: Neel Kashkari: Uncertainty around oil shock means Fed should acknowledge risk of rate hikes
articles.summary: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari warned that uncertainty around oil supply shocks from a potential U.S.-backed conflict with Iran could force the Federal Reserve to raise interest rates multiple times to control inflation. Kashkari cited this as the reason for his dissent at the Fed's recent meeting. Global oil price volatility has broader implications for inflation and economic stability, which could indirectly affect India's policy decisions on energy and monetary measures.
articles.raw_summary: The U.S.-backed war with Iran could change the inflation outlook enough to force the Federal Reserve into "potentially a series" of rate hikes to defend the central bank's 2% inflation target, Minneapolis Fed president Neel Kashkari ‌said in explaining ⁠his dissent ⁠at this week's Fed meeting.

The reserves had previously hit a record high in February.

articles.title: Plastic litter and ghost nets threaten olive ridley hatchlings
articles.summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha is a critical nesting site for olive ridley sea turtles, with nearly 700,000 turtles arriving in February 2025 for mass nesting. Plastic waste and discarded fishing nets (ghost nets) pose severe threats to hatchlings, risking their survival. The article underscores the urgent need for conservation efforts to protect this endangered species and its habitat.
articles.raw_summary: The Rushikulya rookery in Odisha’s Ganjam district is among the most important turtle rookeries in the world. Every year, hundreds of thousands of olive ridley sea turtles (Lepidochelys olivacea) converge here to lay eggs in a synchronous mass nesting event or arribada, with almost 700,000 olive ridleys coming ashore to lay eggs in February 2025 alone. The ruling highlights the legal obligations of digital platforms in addressing fraudulent content.

The order cites concerns over rumors and attempts to sensationalize the case. The action aims to prevent protests and maintain law and order.
articles.raw_summary: UP bans gatherings and condolence visits in Ghazipur under Section 163 of BNSS after OBC teen girl’s death, citing rumours and opposition attempts to sensationalise the case.

The census is a government-led exercise to gather demographic and socio-economic data, which informs policy planning and resource allocation.

articles.title: e-RaktKosh to get new app: Online donor registration, blood tracking to go digital
articles.summary: Rajasthan will launch a new app integrated with e-RaktKosh, India's national blood management system, to enable online blood donor registration, barcode tracking of blood bags, GPS monitoring, and improved management of blood donation camps. articles.title: Strait of Hormuz logs big traffic dip by over 90%, fewer than 10 ships daily amid West Asia flare-up
articles.summary: The UK Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) reported over 40 incidents involving merchant ships in the Strait of Hormuz between March 1 and April 27, including attacks, vessel damage, and harassment. Traffic in the strait has dropped by over 90%, with fewer than 10 ships passing daily amid rising tensions in West Asia. This disruption could impact global oil supplies and shipping routes, raising concerns for India's energy imports and maritime security.

articles.raw_summary: The U.S. Navy’s siege is revealing a hole in Tehran’s strategy of guerrilla warfare and controlling the Strait of Hormuz.
